methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.3B - $5.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Form Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $3.5B

data.narrative:
TAE Technologies is a Series G climate and energy company founded by Rostoker and Binderbauer, currently valued at $6.0B post-money following $1.3B+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies across climate and energy: one public (LNZA) and eight private firms including Form Energy, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Redwood Materials, and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods apply to TAE's valuation. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $6.0B last post-money valuation and applying a typical Series G-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.25x, implies $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Helion at $5.4B (Series F), Form Energy at $3.5B (Series G), and Sila Nanotechnologies at $3.3B (late stage)—yields a median of $3.5B, with a range of $3.3B to $5.4B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $3.0B to $8.6B, with a median of $5.5B. The current $6.0B valuation sits modestly above the peer median, consistent with TAE's stage and capital-raised profile but in the upper half of the implied range. Key uncertainties include the limited comparable set at Series G stage in frontier energy technology and whether stage-progression multiples appropriately capture venture-backed climate tech trajectory.
